Learn more about Configuring the external objectstore or reach out to support.

entries.feature: Integration Service entry in the Automation Cloud product launcher

patterns: Integration Service entry in the Automation Cloud product launcher

entries.recommended_alternative: management of Integration Service assets is being consolidated into Orchestrator , the central location for managing automation resources in Automation Cloud.
entries.source_text: Integration Service entry in the Automation Cloud product launcher | March 2026 | December 2026 | The management of Integration Service assets is being consolidated into Orchestrator , the central location for managing automation resources in Automation Cloud. This transition began in 2025 and is planned for completion by the end of 2026: April 2025 - Event trigger management moved to Orchestrator. March 2026 - Connection management moved to Orchestrator. Later in 2026 - The Connector Builder experience will move to Studio Web . After the transition is complete, the Integration Service entry will be removed from the product launcher in Automation Cloud.

entries.feature: Custom (password-based) authentication for the Snowflake connector

patterns: Custom (password-based) authentication for the Snowflake connector

entries.recommended_alternative: users and removes password access for service accounts, rolling out between August and October 2026.
entries.source_text: Custom (password-based) authentication for the Snowflake connector | July 8, 2026 | July 20, 2026 | Snowflake's Phase 3 strong authentication rollout enforces multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all human users and removes password access for service accounts, rolling out between August and October 2026. Ahead of this enforcement, the Custom authentication method will be removed from the Snowflake connector in Integration Service starting with July 20, 2026 . Existing connections using Custom authentication will continue working until Snowflake applies enforcement to your account. Recreate them using one of the following authentication methods: OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code — recommended for human users RSA Key Pair — recommended for service/unattended scenarios Personal Access Token — alternative for programmatic access For setup instructions, refer to Snowflake authentication in the Integration Service user guide.

Templates will remain available in Studio Web as they are today. Existing automations and connections will continue to work as expected.

entries.recommended_alternative: replaced by the union of privileges model.
entries.source_text: The break inheritance settings model | May 2024 | Q1 (for Community and Enterprise cloud environments), October 2025 (for Automation Suite environments) | The current break inheritance settings model will be replaced by the union of privileges model. The new model will propagate any adjustments made at the group level to all users within the group. The settings impacted by the new model are: UI Profile settings (No UI access, Personal Workspace only, Standard Interface) Update policy settings Enable user to run automations Create a personal workspaces for this user Ensure that you migrate to the new model as soon as it is announced in this release note . The deprecation is only planned for the cloud and Automation Suite environments.

entries.recommended_alternative: migrate any remaining mixed roles.
entries.source_text: Mixed roles | November 2024 | April 2025 | We recommend that you avoid using mixed roles in access configurations. By the time the deprecation is complete, we will migrate any remaining mixed roles. Warning: Once mixed roles are deprecated, the following API calls will fail: Any GET call that retrieves a role by name, if it refers to the name of a deprecated mixed role. Any POST call to the /odata/Users endpoint, if the request payload includes a mixed role assignment. We strongly recommend that you migrate all your automations that currently use the affected endpoints. To maintain equivalent privileges, take the following steps: Replace the Administrator role with the following roles: The Orchestrator Administrator role. Note: The Orchestrator Administrator role also includes the permissions of the Solution Administrator role. The Folder Administrator role. Replace the Robot role with the following roles: The tenant-level Allow to be Automation User role; The folder-level Automation User role. Note: We recommend this approach only for users who have not modified the Robot role. Users who have created custom versions of the Robot role should determine the most suitable way to divide it into tenant and folder roles to accommodate their permission levels. To fully transition from mixed roles, divide existing roles into tenant- and folder-scoped roles, as needed.

entries.recommended_alternative: Review UiPath migration guidance for this server-side feature.
entries.source_text: Classic folders | October 2021 | October 2022 | We recommend migrating to modern folders within these deadlines: *before the middle of May 2023 if you are using Orchestrator in Automation Cloud *before upgrading to 2023.4 if you are using standalone or Automation Suite Orchestrator

entries.source_text: Testing Module in Orchestrator | November 11, 2025 | June 30, 2026 | As of January 1, 2026, the Testing module in Orchestrator will not display any test results anymore. In addition, it will not be possible to execute tests or test sets from Orchestrator. To consume test results and execute, it is required to use Test Manager. Test Set creation and execution in Orchestrator will be removed, with Test Manager becoming the sole platform for creating and executing test sets. This change will take effect on June 30, 2026.

entries.recommended_alternative: replaced by the union of privileges model.
entries.source_text: The break inheritance settings model | May 2024 | Q1 (for Community and Enterprise cloud environments), October 2025 (for Automation Suite environments) | The current break inheritance settings model will be replaced by the union of privileges model. The new model will propagate any adjustments made at the group level to all users within the group. The settings impacted by the new model are: UI Profile settings (No UI access, Personal Workspace only, Standard Interface) Update policy settings Enable user to run automations Create a personal workspaces for this user Ensure that you migrate to the new model as soon as it is announced in this release note . The deprecation is only planned for the cloud and Automation Suite environments. The removal occurs in one of the following scenarios: You migrate to the new union of privileges model via the migration tool. You are automatically migrated in at least three months after we implement the migration tool for transitioning to the union of privileges model. Support for mixed roles will be removed in Orchestrator with the Automation Suite 2.2510.0 release . Mixed role permissions and mixed role assignments will be migrated automatically.

For other deployment options, see the Orchestrator Installation Guide .

entries.source_text: Support for Apache Airflow versions older than Airflow 2.9.0. | Process Mining 2024.10.3 release notes Process Mining 2023.10.9 release notes | November 11, 2025 (Process Mining on Automation Suite 2.2510) | This applies to both new installations and existing installations that upgrade to 2.2510. Note: For the AutomationSuite_Airflow database, Microsoft SQL Server is no longer supported in newer Airflow versions and will require PostgreSQL databases.

entries.recommended_alternative: Use the end of upload API .
entries.source_text: Marker files marker.csv | Process Mining Cloud Release Notes - May 2023 | November 2023 (Cloud) (Automation Suite n/a) | Use the end of upload API . See Upload data using an extractor .

entries.recommended_alternative: use TRACY for the process graph layout.
entries.source_text: Process graph layout | Process Mining Cloud Release Notes - February 2023 | October 2023 (Cloud), Process Mining 2023.10 (Automation Suite) | With the introduction of TRACY, all process apps will use TRACY for the process graph layout. Existing process apps using the previous process graph layout will no longer work in Process Mining (Cloud) and Process Mining 2023.10 (Automation Suite). Recreate or migrate your process apps before installing Process Mining 2023.10 , to make sure the process apps are still accessible. See Migrating apps for the new process graph layout .

entries.recommended_alternative: use HTTPS.
entries.source_text: HTTP (unsecure) requests through NuGet feeds | February 2025 | October 2025 | Starting with the Robot releases in February 2025 (for the latest versions) and October 2025 (for LTS versions), NuGet-supported packages no longer allow calls to HTTP external feeds. This type of feeds can be configured in Orchestrator or in the NuGet.config file . Note that built-in Orchestrator feeds always use HTTPS. Using HTTP results in errors, and opting out of this behavior is not possible. This change is part of an ongoing effort to enforce HTTPS usage across NuGet client experiences . Transition to HTTPS feeds to avoid disruptions.

entries.recommended_alternative: Manager standalone deprecation | March 2026 | October 2026 | We would like to inform you that Test Manager 2025.
entries.source_text: Test Manager standalone deprecation | March 2026 | October 2026 | We would like to inform you that Test Manager 2025.10 (standalone) was the final standalone release of Test Manager. There will be no standalone Test Manager 2026.10 release . What this means for you 2025.10 is the last standalone version of Test Manager. If you are using the standalone edition, you should plan your transition strategy accordingly. Continued innovation and enhancements will focus on the integrated delivery models. We are committed to making this transition as smooth as possible. We encourage you to transition to Test Cloud by adhering to the platform migration instructions and the artefact migration instructions . If you have questions, please reach out to your UiPath representative.
